description: In 2011, Coca-Cola launched the 'Share a Coke' campaign in Australia, replacing their logo on bottles with popular first names to encourage user-generated content and personal connections. The strategy triggered a massive viral surge of consumers sharing photos of their namesake bottles on social media, significantly reversing a decade-long decline in youth consumption.

key_events.description: Coca-Cola Australia introduces personalized labels in physical retail stores.
Social media saturation begins as users post #ShareACoke photos across Facebook and Twitter.
National news outlets begin reporting on the 'crazes' and shortages of specific names in stores.
Independent marketing analysts release data showing a 7% increase in youth consumption directly linked to the campaign.

calibration_notes: This scenario calibrates how a brand-led user-generated content campaign shifts from initial curiosity to mass viral adoption, measuring the speed at which undecided segments convert into brand advocates.
